score_components.reason: Video is instructional/educational content with narration overlays, 3D anatomical illustrations, and educational text slides — NOT a trainee or expert performance video. No actual FLS task execution is demonstrated. No instruments, FLS trainer box, or foam model visible. Cannot score per rubric.

confidence_rationale: Video classification is unambiguous. Content consists entirely of medical educational illustrations, anatomy diagrams, and instructional text from Nucleus Medical Media. No laparoscopic performance, timer, or FLS equipment is present.
cannot_determine: All performance metrics — video is not a performance recording

demonstrated_techniques: Anatomical target identification for tubal ligation
Laparoscopic approach and visualization of reproductive appendages
Correct positioning of ligating loop at appendage base
Loop cinching and knot deployment mechanics
Post-ligation tissue appearance and confirmation of secure ligation
common_errors_addressed: Incorrect appendage identification (anatomy review emphasizes middle appendage as target)
Off-mark ligation (illustrations show knot placement at marked site near appendage base)
Inadequate loop security (post-procedure images show firmly seated, darkened loop indicating secure cinch)
teaching_content.context: This is a comprehensive patient and trainee education resource on laparoscopic tubal ligation. It covers clinical context (birth control methods), pre-operative preparation, intra-operative visualization, correct technique, and post-operative care. Suitable for trainee knowledge foundation prior to hands-on FLS task practice.

recommendation: This resource is appropriate for introducing task3_endoloop concept and anatomy but does NOT substitute for actual FLS trainer performance assessment. Trainees should practice on the FLS trainer box with foam appendages, endoloop equipment, and laparoscopic instruments to be evaluated on task completion time, loop placement accuracy, and secure cinching per official rubric.
